changes.before: His gaze stuck on their speed, their faces, the bow on Sylvanas's back.
changes.after: He watched their speed, their faces, and the bow on Sylvanas's back.

changes.before: Jaina kept her eyes ahead.
changes.after: Jaina faced forward.

changes.before: The answer came too fast.
She stayed with the useful part because the south road needed that more than it needed confession.
changes.after: She stayed with the useful part; there was no time for confession on the ride south.

changes.before: Jaina looked straight ahead and wished for hedges, trees, anything but flat country and air.
changes.after: Jaina wished for hedges, trees, anything but flat country and air.

changes.before: That at least meant he had stopped pretending the Council still had time for polite upholstery.
changes.after: That meant he had stopped wasting time on politeness.

changes.before: Jaina steadied her mare with her knees and spoke into the stone while the road moved under them.
changes.after: Jaina steadied her mare with her knees and spoke into the stone while the horses kept moving.

changes.before: Silence came back over the crystal.
changes.after: Antonidas did not answer at once.

changes.before: Jaina looked at the red-gold line of sun caught in a ditch.
changes.after: A red-gold line of sun burned in the ditch.

changes.before: He asked as her teacher, not her archmage.
changes.after: He asked as her teacher.

changes.before: He asked as her teacher, not her archmage.
changes.after: He asked as her teacher.

changes.before: The mare stepped around a stone and for one strange second the whole road tilted anyway.
changes.after: The mare stepped around a stone and for one strange second she still felt the world tilt.

changes.before: It only made the refusal hurt somewhere else.
Only the old angle of his dry impatience showed through, long enough to remind her he was still himself under all the pressure.
changes.after: For a moment Antonidas sounded dry and impatient again.

changes.before: "No," she said, and almost managed the shape of a smile. "It isn't."
changes.after: "No," she said, and managed a thin smile. "It isn't."

changes.before: Jaina turned then, because the words demanded it.
changes.after: Jaina turned then, because she could not ignore the accusation.

changes.before: Sylvanas nodded once.
changes.after: "Very well."

changes.before: Ordinary Lordaeron work spread over a kingdom already baking its own knife.
changes.after: Ordinary Lordaeron work went on while poisoned grain moved through the kingdom.

changes.before: Sylvanas looked across at her then, fully for the first time since the road began.
changes.after: Sylvanas turned to her then, fully for the first time since the road began.

changes.before: Jaina looked south.
changes.after: Jaina faced south.

changes.before: Sylvanas held her gaze another second.
changes.after: Sylvanas stayed turned toward her for another second.

changes.before: Being right alone looked too much like Kel'Thuzad: a desk, a closed room, a man pleased to find himself mirrored.
changes.after: Kel'Thuzad had worked alone in a closed room, pleased with himself.

changes.before: The shape of it demanded something else.
changes.after: She needed something else from it.

changes.before: It was still something.
The half-step was still there: trust under strain, still working, still checking every gap.
changes.after: Sylvanas still kept the half-step, close enough to listen and far enough to watch.

changes.before: That would have to be enough for this road.
For now, it was.

Jaina kept her gaze ahead because if she shut her eyes she would see Antonidas at a library table in Dalaran, sleeves inked at the cuff, pushing a dangerous text across to her because he wanted the pleasure of watching her understand it.
changes.after: Jaina kept facing forward because if she shut her eyes she would see Antonidas at a library table in Dalaran, sleeves inked at the cuff, pushing a dangerous text across to her because he wanted the pleasure of watching her understand it.
